name: Excessive Login Failures via Network Connection to Multiple Machines
notes:
This rule triggers when multiple failed authentication events on different machines from a single source IP address are detected.
This behaviour indicates a potential brute-force attempt to access multiple machines.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 10 times with the same Source Address and different Destination Address in 5 minutes
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Login Failure
when the event matches Logon Type (custom) is any of 3
when BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 10 times with the same Source Address and different Destination Address in 5 minutes
thresholds: when BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 10 times with the same Source Address and different Destination Address in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Excessive Login Failures via Network Connection to Multiple Machines on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Login Failure
AND when the event matches Logon Type (custom) is any of 3
AND when BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 10 times with the same Source Address and different Destination Address in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Excessive Windows Login Failures via Network Connection to Multiple Machines
Event description: Multiple Windows Login Failures viaNetwork Connection have been seen to Multiple Machines
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
